description: Runnable AANA adapter examples that show how one domain workflow maps into constraints, verifiers, correction actions, and gate results.

adapters.title: Budgeted Travel Planning
adapters.workflow: Plan an outing while preserving budget, transport, lunch, day-count, and destination constraints.
best_for: plans with forbidden transport or required inclusions
adapters.prompt: Plan a one-day San Diego museum outing for two adults with a hard $110 total budget, public transit only, lunch included, and no single ticket above $25.
adapters.bad_candidate: Use rideshare, buy a $40 ticket, and spend $150 total.
adapters.copy_command: python scripts/run_adapter.py --adapter examples/travel_adapter.json --prompt 'Plan a one-day San Diego museum outing for two adults with a hard $110 total budget, public transit only, lunch included, and no single ticket above $25.' --candidate 'Use rideshare, buy a $40 ticket, and spend $150 total.'
caveats: Production use needs live prices, hours, route feasibility, and accessibility data.

adapters.title: Budgeted Allergy-Safe Meal Planning
adapters.workflow: Create a meal plan while preserving grocery budget, dietary exclusions, diet requirements, and requested day coverage.
best_for: weekly meal plans with explicit budget accounting
adapters.prompt: Create a weekly gluten-free, dairy-free meal plan for one person with a $70 grocery budget.
adapters.bad_candidate: Buy regular pasta, wheat bread, cheese, and milk for $95 total. Monday: pasta. Tuesday: cheese sandwiches.
adapters.copy_command: python scripts/run_adapter.py --adapter examples/meal_planning_adapter.json --prompt 'Create a weekly gluten-free, dairy-free meal plan for one person with a $70 grocery budget.' --candidate 'Buy regular pasta, wheat bread, cheese, and milk for $95 total. Monday: pasta. Tuesday: cheese sandwiches.'
caveats: Production use needs current prices, package labels, availability, and user-specific allergy guidance.
